title: sutera microphylla
intro: Sutera microphylla, also known as the narrow-leaved snailflower or blue pimpernel, is a flowering plant species in the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sutera microphylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource in the treatment of various health conditions. Sutera microphylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, or used in the form of extracts and essential oils, which can be applied topically or ingested under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
